task: Run first Rust-default self-play mix arena experiment under S0003.

Runtime got worse than expected because the current JS Rust backend shells out to the Rust eval binary per search/evaluation and reloads the artifact repeatedly.

task: Run S0003 after moving self-play and arena loops into native Rust binaries.

Self-play logs were not visible in the returned run tail because they occurred early and were pushed out by later Python training/arena/check output; durable log files are needed.

task: Try feature cache, Rust-side cache prewarming, parallel candidate weights, and tinygrad availability for S0003 self-play mix training speed.
outcome: Kept infrastructure improvement. The S0003 Rust self-play mix sweep completed in 17.2s with checks passing, versus prior timeouts around 1136s. Added disk-backed Python frozen-conv feature cache, a Rust feature-cache prewarmer using the Rust frozen-conv implementation, and parallel candidate weight training/arena evaluation. tinygrad is not installed in this environment, so no tinygrad path was enabled yet. Primary metric was 0.75 with zero illegal losses; however best weight remained 0 and all arena games were value-adjudicated, so this is mostly an infrastructure/speed validation rather than proof of self-play mix strength.

task: Install/expose tinygrad GPU backend on the RTX 3090 and rerun the S0003 tinygrad A/B.
outcome: Kept infrastructure. User-space CUDA packages were installed into .venv-tinygrad and dovetail.sh now exports CUDA_HOME/PATH/LD_LIBRARY_PATH for tinygrad. tinygrad now selects the NV backend and runs on the NVIDIA RTX 3090. Single-candidate cached trainer time improved from tinygrad DEV=PYTHON ~50.1s to tinygrad CUDA ~5.43s. Full S0003 tinygrad CUDA sweep completed in 21.97s with checks passing, but the cached pure-Python parallel baseline is still faster at 17.2s for this tiny dataset. Metric stayed 0.75, best weight stayed 0, and all arena games were value-adjudicated.
